next_step_text: 6/26 BCE: Reached out to Nicole, SVP of IT 6/17 Going to offer a live netsuite integration 6/15 BCE: In limbo. Someone from the Conga team viewed the demo. 5/30 BCE: Andy has gone quiet. Still pushing 5/8 BCE: Demo occurred. Went well. Following up with Andy on next steps. Seems like implementation/integration call is next step 5/1 BCE: RFP Submitted. Demo next week 4/20 BCE: Met with Andy again.

recent_activities_14d.subject: [Clari - Email Sent] For Nicole - Zycus Follow Up

our_open_deliverables.text: Send slide deck on integrations, implementations, and workflow-builder screenshots; schedule follow-up meeting with head of implementations
Send details on Zylow integration and spend-analytics demo materials
Deliver preliminary quote template and phased implementation pricing once user counts are returned
Deliver ROI/business case justification for $150K S2P spend post-PROS acquisition, anchored to merger synergy targets or cost-reduction mandate

buyer_dependent_items.text: Send mutual NDA to Bailey Erazo to enable RFP participation
Respond to rep's live NetSuite integration offer and confirm availability for integration/implementation scoping call
Introduce Economic Buyer, CFO, or C-level sponsor to validate $150K budget and decision authority
Return the completed user-count input form to enable accurate pricing

competitive_position.summary: The single strongest CURRENT threat is the status quo (extend NetSuite, do nothing) given Andy's silence and post-PROS budget scrutiny; no named vendor shortlist has been disclosed. Coupa and Zip are inferred from Andy's usage history, not confirmed active bidders. Zycus's recency-weighted advantage is full-suite breadth, native Teams UI, and Zycus-delivered implementation.

competitors.name: Status quo (NetSuite-only manual P2P)
competitors.quote: right now we only use NetSuite. We have shoehorned NetSuite into a procure-to-pay
competitors.how_we_win: Quantify the cost of manual intake and lost-sourcing-savings to make change cheaper than standing still post-PROS

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.source: Avoma intro 12 Dec 2025 (Andy named tools he has used; not confirmed in RFP)
competitors.how_we_win: Position full intake-to-pay suite plus Merlin autonomous negotiation as deeper than Coupa, with native Teams front door

competitors.name: Zip
competitors.quote: when I was at Procore, we did implement intake to procure with Zip
competitors.how_we_win: Zip lacks true esourcing ('I don't believe Zip has esourcing'); lead with esourcing + spend analytics breadth

competitors.name: Ivalua
competitors.quote: no confirmed mention on a Conga call (seen only on an unrelated 17 Mar BakerRipley RFP touchpoint)
competitors.source: Avoma touchpoint 17 Mar 2026 (different account, not Conga)
competitors.how_we_win: Not a confirmed Conga competitor; do not over-weight

competitors.name: Unknown shortlist
competitors.quote: Zycus amongst a few other players
competitors.how_we_win: Ask Andy directly for shortlist size and how Zycus is scored when he re-engages; lead the live NetSuite integration proof to pull ahead on technical fit

champion_strength.source: RFP Invitation 13 Apr; Avoma intro 12 Dec 2025; SF Next Step 30 May / 15 Jun
champion_strength.summary: Andy Simmons sponsored the RFP and shaped criteria toward Zycus, but his ~31-day silence and failure to introduce the budget owner make the champion at-risk and unproven at the power level.

meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer mapped or engaged in 6.5 months. Andy said on 12 Dec he has 'a lot of people behind this up to the C-level' and 'I'm not doing this on my own,' and the 6 May demo confirmed a CFO sits in the approval chain ('anything above $1,000 goes to you and the CFO'), but that person is unnamed and unengaged. The 26 Jun outreach to Nicole Gallego (SVP Delivery Ops & IT) is the first reach toward a more senior buyer. Because Andy authored the RFP, the path to power runs THROUGH him (or through Nicole if she engages) — broker the introduction, do not cold-outreach a CFO. This is the single highest-leverage gap and the reason Best Case cannot stand.

recommended_moves: Convert the offered live NetSuite integration into a BOOKED technical scoping session — propose two concrete dates and name the Zycus implementations lead; route through Nicole Gallego (SVP IT) and cc Andy.

Re-arm champion Andy with a tight re-engagement note proposing the same integration dates and asking him to broker the budget owner into the session ('so we scope the right phasing/investment for your leadership').
Co-build a one-page quantified ROI case (manual-intake cost, sourcing-savings target, AP cycle-time) anchored to post-PROS synergy, so Andy or the budget owner can defend the $150K spend.
